title: nuxt-i18n-mcp project configuration
description: Configuration file for the nuxt-i18n-mcp MCP server. Place as .i18n-mcp.json at your Nuxt project root (next to nuxt.config.ts). All fields are optional — the server passes them to the AI agent as context for translation workflows.

$schema.description: Path or URL to the JSON schema for IDE autocompletion.

context.description: Free-form project background for the agent — business domain, user base, brand voice, anything that helps the agent understand the project.

layerRules.description: Rules that help the agent decide which Nuxt layer a new translation key belongs to. The agent interprets the natural-language 'when' field.

layer.description: Layer name as it appears in the Nuxt config (e.g., 'root', 'app-admin', 'app-shop').

description.description: What this layer contains — key namespaces, domain scope, etc.

when.description: Natural-language rule describing when a key should go in this layer.

glossary.description: Term dictionary for consistent translations. Keys are source terms, values describe the required translation or usage note.

translationPrompt.description: System prompt prepended to all translation requests (both MCP sampling and agent-inline fallback). Sets tone, style, and constraints.

localeNotes.description: Per-locale context included in translation prompts. Keys are locale codes (e.g., 'de-DE', 'en-US', 'de-DE-formal'), values describe register, regional conventions, or other locale-specific guidance.

examples.description: Few-shot translation examples that demonstrate the project's style. The agent uses these as reference when generating translations.

items.description: A single translation example. Must have a 'key' property. All other properties are locale codes (e.g., 'de-DE', 'en-US') mapped to their translated value, or 'note' with a style comment.

note.description: Optional style or context note about this example.

orphanScan.description: Per-layer scan directories for orphan key detection. In monorepos, a shared layer's keys may be used across multiple apps. Map each layer name to the directories that should be scanned for its key usage. Paths are relative to the project root.

description.description: Optional description of why these directories are scanned for this layer.

scanDirs.description: Directories to scan for key usage (relative to project root).
